questions: Identify the major digital resource produced by a joint IUSSP and UNFPA project that explicitly positions itself as following in a direct line of descent from the UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this resource, locate the specific chapter or section dedicated to the 'Evaluation and correction of fertility data.' Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, ensuring you include the author, the year of publication, the chapter title, the title of the overarching volume, and the complete list of editors.

It then applies Wide aggregation by requesting specific bibliographic details (citation components) found on a specific sub-page within that resource.

rounds.answer_a:
The major digital resource following UN Manual X is the *Training and Research Resource on Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation*, an IUSSP and UNFPA collaborative project launched in 2024. The specific section on "Evaluation and correction of fertility data" constitutes Chapter 4 of this online manual. It was authored by Thomas W. Pullum.

The full suggested citation is:

Pullum, T.W. (2024). Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In T.A. Moultrie, R.E. Dorrington, A.G. Hill, K. Hill, I.M. Timæus, and B. Zaba (Eds.), *Training and Research Resource on Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation*. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP), and New York: United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

## References

[1] IUSSP and UNFPA. (2024). *Training and Research Resource on Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation*. Retrieved from the IUSSP-UNFPA Training and Research Resource website: https://demographicestimation.iussp.org

[2] Pullum, T.W. (2024). Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In T.A. Moultrie, R.E. Dorrington, A.G. Hill, K. Hill, I.M. Timæus, and B. Zaba (Eds.), *Training and Research Resource on Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation*. Paris: IUSSP, and New York: UNFPA. Retrieved from https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/fertility-evaluation/Pullum_EvaluationInput_4.pdf
rounds.answer_b:
The major digital resource is the online publication *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, known as the “IUSSP Demographic Estimation Manual” or “IUSSP DemEst Manual.” It is a joint project of the International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP) and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), explicitly described as the successor to the UN’s *Manual X*. The specific chapter for evaluating fertility data is Chapter 6, titled “Evaluation and adjustment of fertility data.” Its suggested full citation is: Moultrie, T. (2013). Evaluation and adjustment of fertility data. In *Tools for Demographic Estimation*, edited by T. Moultrie, R. Dorrington, A. Hill, K. Hill, I. Timæus, and B. Zaba. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. The manual is an open-access online resource, with its content mirroring the structure of prior UN manuals but updated with new methods and software applications for demographic estimation [1][2]. The editors listed are the key demographers who oversaw the entire volume’s development [1].

## References
[1] International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. (n.d.). Tools for Demographic Estimation. Retrieved from https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/
[2] Moultrie, T. A., et al. (Eds.). (2013). *Tools for Demographic Estimation*. IUSSP.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B correctly identifies the resource (*Tools for Demographic Estimation*), the correct author (Moultrie), and the correct list of editors. Agent A hallucinates the author (attributing it to Thomas Pullum, who is associated with DHS reports, not this specific IUSSP manual chapter) and hallucinates a 2024 publication date. While Agent A has better formatting, the information is factually incorrect regarding the primary request (the citation).
